## Claude responded: Love it!

Love it! Let me build out a full visual direction for a playful Gen Z social/community app.

Log in to see interactive visuals

Here's your visual direction breakdown — three distinct options with the middle one, **Electric Nostalgia**, flagged as the strongest fit for a Gen Z social app.

The thinking behind it: Gen Z responds to bold, expressive UI that feels distinctly _theirs_ — loud but intentional, with retro digital references (Y2K color pops, tight typography, high contrast) mixed with modern flat sensibility. Hyper Violet + Acid Peach is that balance of chaotic and cool.

The other two directions are real alternatives depending on your angle — Soft Bubble if you want something warmer and more emotionally safe (think mental health or support communities), and Chill Zine if your community has a creative/indie bent.

Hit any of the buttons to go deeper — especially the UI mockup one if you're ready to see this in an actual interface.



## Claude responded: Soft Bubble is a lovely direction — it naturally builds trust and warmth, which matters a lot for community apps where people need to feel safe enough to actua…

Log in to see interactive visuals

Soft Bubble is a lovely direction — it naturally builds trust and warmth, which matters a lot for community apps where people need to feel safe enough to actually show up and participate.

A few things worth noting about this palette in practice: the lavender + bubblegum combo works best when you keep backgrounds very light (Blush and Lilac Mist), so the brighter accent colors land with impact rather than feeling noisy. And Nunito is doing a lot of heavy lifting here — its rounded terminals are what give the whole thing that "pillow" feel, so don't swap it out for something more angular or the vibe breaks.

Hit any of the buttons below the moodboard to keep going — the UI mockup one is a great next step if you want to see how this actually feels inside a screen.



## Claude responded: I can build you a YouTube-style app called AmaniTube!

I can build you a YouTube-style app called **AmaniTube**! Let me create that for you.

Log in to see interactive visuals

Here's your **AmaniTube** app! It's fully interactive — here's what you can do:

- **Home feed** — browse video cards in the grid, click any to open the player
- **Video player** — click the play button, scrub the progress bar, like videos, and subscribe to channels
- **Sidebar navigation** — switch between Home, Explore, Subscriptions, and Library
- **Category chips** — filter by Music, Gaming, Vlogs, Tech, etc.
- **Up next** — related videos shown below the player

The design uses the Soft Bubble palette we built (lavender, pink, mint, peach) to give AmaniTube its own identity. Want me to customize anything — like the app name styling, add a dark mode, add a comments section, or change the color scheme?



## Claude responded: Got it!

Got it! Adding an upload/create feature with a 30MB file limit.

Log in to see interactive visuals

The **Create / Upload** feature is now added! Here's how it works:

- Click the purple **Create** button in the top nav (or "Upload video" in the sidebar)
- **Drag & drop** a video file or click **Choose file** to browse
- Files over **30 MB** are instantly rejected with a clear error message
- Non-video files are also blocked
- A **progress bar** simulates the upload
- Fill in the title, description, category, visibility, and tags
- Hit **Publish video** and you'll get a success notification

Want me to add anything else — like a thumbnail picker, comments section, or a dark mode toggle?
